patches.replace:
Sign-Only Predictions: A Recurring Non-Discriminating Defense Pattern

Three V51 confirmations (NMP deceleration sector, SAA westward cell shift, NH heat excess) share a structural defect: when the dome’s quantitative derivation fails or is absent, a defender retreats to “we predicted the SIGN, not the magnitude.” But standard physics independently predicts the same sign through unrelated mechanisms, so a sign-match is non-discriminating between the two models. Each per-WIN defense becomes a coin flip and collapses the confirmation.

Three V51 WINs sit in the same defensive posture once their quantitative content is removed. WIN-059 (“NMP deceleration Siberian”) predicts a sector-specific slowdown of the magnetic pole’s northward drift; the dome’s axially symmetric H(r) = 8,537·exp(−r/8,619) is mechanically incapable of producing sector-specific behaviour, yet the same observed deceleration is derived quantitatively by Livermore et al. (2020) from changing Siberian-versus-Canadian flux-lobe intensities — a globe-MHD mechanism whose rate falls out of core-flow inversion. WIN-060 (“SAA western cell shift”) cites the cell’s westward drift as confirmation; westward drift has been a published observable since Halley’s 1701 declination chart, and Finlay et al. (2016) derives the modern rate (~0.2°/yr) from core-mantle-boundary flow inversions. WIN-066 (“NH heat excess”) cites the +0.34 W/m²/decade trend; Loeb et al. (2025) attributes the trend to differential land-mass distribution (~39% NH vs ~19% SH), albedo asymmetry, and aerosol/cloud forcing. In each case, both the dome and the standard model predict the same direction. The standard model also produces the quantitative magnitude; the dome does not.

The defender pivot. Once a dome reviewer notes the missing magnitude — for WIN-059, a 39.9% error against the dome’s own NMP-drift Test 6; for WIN-066, the ~250–800× over-prediction from the inverse-cube integral of h = 5,733 km dome geometry — a defender can pivot to: “We never claimed the magnitude. The dome predicted the SIGN, and the sign matches.” This collapses the WIN onto its weakest content. The pivot succeeds only if the sign is informative. It is not. A coin flip would land on the predicted sign half the time; standard physics lands on it for documented mechanistic reasons. The Bayesian update from a sign-match is therefore zero or near-zero — the same observation is equally expected under both hypotheses, so it cannot distinguish them.

WIN-058 borderline. WIN-058 (“Unified angular coordinate θ = −lonE”) is an adjacent member of this family rather than a directional match per se: its claim is a coordinate identity that follows from the definition of geographic longitude, true for any polar-azimuthal projection regardless of dome physics. We classified that one “Misleading” rather than “Std Model Explains” because it is closer to definitional circularity than to sign-only confirmation; the family resemblance is that both shapes of WIN survive falsification not by being right but by being structurally untestable. Section 2.9 covers the coordinate-loop pattern in depth.

Three counters that close the pivot for any new WIN. The WIN-066 detail_verdict_text now carries the closure paragraph for that specific case. The three counters generalize, and a curmudgeon reviewing any future sign-only WIN should apply them in order. (1) Independent sign-derivation under the standard model: if mainstream physics derives the same sign from unrelated mechanisms (Siberian flux dynamics, CMB flow inversion, land-mass-asymmetry forcing), the sign is non-discriminating regardless of the dome’s claim. (2) Cited value commits the dome to magnitude: when the dome’s WIN cites a specific number (a drift rate, a westward shift coordinate, a +0.34 W/m²/decade), the WIN is staked on that number — disclaiming the magnitude post-hoc is a retreat from the WIN’s own framing, not a clarification of it. (3) Sign-only with no derivation is a 50/50 prior: a model that predicts only sign, with no derivation, has the same falsifiability content as a coin flip — confirmation is information-free. Either the dome predicts a magnitude derived from its parameters (and is wrong by ~250–800× on WIN-066, 39.9% on WIN-059, or has no derivation at all on WIN-060), or it predicts only sign (and any positive value would have confirmed, which is not confirmation in any operational sense).

Anticipated counter-pivot — “you are using globe mechanisms to refute a dome model.” A sophisticated defender will note that Counter (1) invokes globe-model mechanisms (Siberian flux lobes, MHD core flow, NH continental albedo) to argue that the sign is non-discriminating, and may protest: “of course the standard model predicts the same sign — you assume the standard model.” Two replies. First, the underlying observables in those mechanisms — continental land-mass percentages, observatory readings of the geomagnetic field, CERES top-of-atmosphere energy fluxes — are direct measurements independent of the geometry dispute. The standard model derives the magnitudes from those observables; the dome adopts the numbers. The mechanism is not “imported”; it is the only derivation on the table. Second, this defender move concedes the point: if the dome’s case for a WIN depends on rejecting the only mechanistic derivation that fits the observation, the dome must publish its own quantitative derivation from its own parameters. Until it does, the WIN is a sign-match against a model the dome rejects, which is not a confirmation of anything.

Auditor rule going forward. When a new WIN is added to the dome’s prediction list and the dome’s quantitative derivation either fails or is absent, the WIN should be re-classified rather than counted as a confirmation. If the dome publishes a value derived from disc radius, firmament height, sun altitude, or moon altitude that matches observation, that is a legitimate confirmation candidate. If the dome adopts an observed value and frames the match as sign-only, that is non-discriminating against the standard model and earns “Std Model Explains” or “Misleading” at intake, not “Confirmed.” The cross-WIN pattern identified here is what makes per-WIN closure rhetorically expensive; naming it once in this subsection lets the per-WIN detail_verdict_texts cross-reference rather than re-derive.
patches.rationale: Insert sign-only-non-discrimination subsection per EXP-337 (cross-WIN sweep for WIN-059/060/066 sign-only defender-pivot pattern). Closes ISS-1926.
